description_long: This is a light, single-propeller airplane with a high-wing configuration and a conventional tail. The fuselage is streamlined and painted in a pale gray color, with the registration 'Nu D38' clearly marked on the wings and side. The aircraft features two fixed landing gear legs with visible wheels and a tailwheel, indicating a taildragger setup. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, likely accommodating one or two seats. The propeller and engine are mounted at the nose, and the overall design suggests a classic, utilitarian aircraft suitable for short-range flights.
description: A pale gray, high-wing, single-propeller airplane with fixed landing gear and a conventional tail, marked 'Nu D38'. The cockpit is forward and enclosed, with a streamlined fuselage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the nose-mounted propeller, forward cockpit, and symmetrical high wings with fixed landing gear visible.
description_view_1: Oblique top-left view highlighting the 'Nu D38' markings on the wing and fuselage, as well as the tail and wing structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tailwheel, conventional tail design, and the full span of the high-mounted wings.
description_view_3: Oblique top-right view showing the right side of the fuselage, wing markings, and the tail section.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit likely configured for one or two seats, with a clear canopy.
